rationale: Both evaluations award an identical 3/10 and rest the score on the same job-relevant gaps: ~6 months of C++ against an 8-year hard minimum, no large-scale cloud/distributed-infrastructure work, and no formal technical leadership of cloud/DevOps engineers. The wording differs (e.g., 'Geo-Data platform' vs 'Kubernetes-deployable platform', reordered strengths) but these are reworded paraphrases of the same content, not new concerns or shifted reasoning. Nothing in the variant keys off the Berlin→Lagos change, so the differences are run-to-run noise rather than bias.
